req.title: Consent management
req.statement: A patient shall grant or revoke consent for data sharing; revoked consent shall be enforced within 15 minutes.
error_pathways: accesses after revoke within SLA denied

doc.reasoning: R-HLT-015 requires three distinct verification areas: (1) a patient can grant consent and it is persisted (positive/EP), (2) a patient can revoke consent and downstream access is denied after the 15-minute SLA window (negative/NEG + state transition), and (3) access attempts made exactly at the 15-minute SLA boundary are correctly enforced (boundary/BVA). Each test is scoped to the /Consent endpoint of fhir-lite, uses a patient-role token fixture, and is idempotent via per-test resource teardown.

tests.name: test_grant_consent_stored_successfully
preconditions: Authenticated patient token available via <patient_auth_token> fixture
No existing Consent resource for this patient-subject pair
actions: POST /Consent with a valid FHIR Consent payload granting data sharing
GET /Consent/{id} and verify status field equals 'active'
expected: Location header points to new resource
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

BASE_URL = "http://localhost:8080/fhir"

@pytest.fixture
def consent_payload():
    return {
        "resourceType": "Consent",
        "status": "active",
        "scope": {"coding": [{"code": "patient-privacy"}]},
        "category": [{"coding": [{"code": "59284-0"}]}],
        "patient": {"reference": "Patient/<test_patient_id>"},
        "provision": {"type": "permit"}
    }

def test_grant_consent_stored_successfully(patient_auth_token, consent_payload):
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {patient_auth_token}",
               "Content-Type": "application/fhir+json"}
    post_resp = requests.post(f"{BASE_URL}/Consent", json=consent_payload, headers=headers)
    assert post_resp.status_code == 201, f"Expected 201, got {post_resp.status_code}"
    body = post_resp.json()
    assert body.get("resourceType") == "Consent"
    assert body.get("provision", {}).get("type") == "permit"
    resource_id = body["id"]
    get_resp = requests.get(f"{BASE_URL}/Consent/{resource_id}", headers=headers)
    assert get_resp.status_code == 200
    assert get_resp.json()["status"] == "active"
    # cleanup
    requests.delete(f"{BASE_URL}/Consent/{resource_id}", headers=headers)

tests.name: test_revoked_consent_denies_access_after_sla
preconditions: Authenticated patient token available via <patient_auth_token> fixture
An active Consent resource exists for the patient (created in setup)
A <downstream_resource_url> exists that is governed by the consent
actions: Assert HTTP 200 or 204 returned on revoke
Wait 15 minutes + 1 second (900 s) for SLA to elapse using <wait_for_sla>
Attempt GET on the downstream governed resource using patient token
expected: After SLA window, downstream GET returns 403 Forbidden or 401 Unauthorized
Response body contains a denial reason referencing consent
trace: R-HLT-015: revoked consent enforced within 15 minutes
R-HLT-015: accesses after revoke within SLA denied
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests
import time

BASE_URL = "http://localhost:8080/fhir"
SLA_SECONDS = 901  # 15 min + 1 s

def test_revoked_consent_denies_access_after_sla(patient_auth_token, active_consent_id):
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {patient_auth_token}",
               "Content-Type": "application/fhir+json"}
    patch_body = [{"op": "replace", "path": "/status", "value": "inactive"}]
    patch_headers = {**headers, "Content-Type": "application/json-patch+json"}
    revoke_resp = requests.patch(
        f"{BASE_URL}/Consent/{active_consent_id}",
        json=patch_body, headers=patch_headers
    )
    assert revoke_resp.status_code in (200, 204), (
        f"Revoke failed: {revoke_resp.status_code}")
    # Wait for SLA to elapse; replace with <wait_for_sla>(SLA_SECONDS) in CI
    time.sleep(SLA_SECONDS)
    governed_url = f"{BASE_URL}/Observation?patient=<test_patient_id>"
    access_resp = requests.get(governed_url, headers=headers)
    assert access_resp.status_code in (401, 403), (
        f"Expected denial after revoke, got {access_resp.status_code}")

tests.name: test_access_denied_exactly_at_sla_boundary
preconditions: Authenticated patient token available via <patient_auth_token> fixture
An active Consent resource exists (created just before test)
System clock is synchronised; time can be controlled via <advance_clock> or waited precisely
Downstream governed resource accessible under the Consent
actions: Record revocation timestamp T0 by PATCHing Consent status to 'inactive'
Immediately attempt GET on governed downstream resource
Assert HTTP 403 or 401 — access must already be denied at the exact SLA boundary
expected: At T0 + 900 s the access attempt is denied (403 or 401)
No successful 200 response is returned at or after the boundary
Response headers or body indicate consent-driven denial
trace: R-HLT-015: revoked consent enforced within 15 minutes (boundary = exactly 15 min)
R-HLT-015: error pathway — accesses after revoke within SLA denied
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests
import time

BASE_URL = "http://localhost:8080/fhir"
SLA_BOUNDARY = 900  # exactly 15 minutes in seconds

def test_access_denied_exactly_at_sla_boundary(patient_auth_token, active_consent_id):
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {patient_auth_token}",
               "Content-Type": "application/json-patch+json"}
    patch_body = [{"op": "replace", "path": "/status", "value": "inactive"}]
    revoke_resp = requests.patch(
        f"{BASE_URL}/Consent/{active_consent_id}",
        json=patch_body, headers=headers
    )
    assert revoke_resp.status_code in (200, 204)
    t0 = time.monotonic()
    elapsed = time.monotonic() - t0
    remaining = SLA_BOUNDARY - elapsed
    if remaining > 0:
        time.sleep(remaining)  # replace with <wait_until>(t0 + SLA_BOUNDARY)
    get_headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {patient_auth_token}"}
    governed_url = f"{BASE_URL}/Observation?patient=<test_patient_id>"
    resp = requests.get(governed_url, headers=get_headers)
    assert resp.status_code in (401, 403), (
        f"Expected denial at SLA boundary (900 s), got {resp.status_code}")

tests.name: test_consent_transitions_active_to_inactive_state
preconditions: Authenticated patient token available via <patient_auth_token> fixture
No pre-existing Consent resource for the patient-subject pair
actions: POST /Consent with status='proposed' — verify 201 and state = proposed
PUT /Consent/{id} to transition status to 'active' — verify 200
PATCH /Consent/{id} to transition status to 'inactive' — verify 200 or 204
GET /Consent/{id} and confirm final status is 'inactive'
expected: No intermediate state allows unauthorised access during transition
trace: R-HLT-015: consent stored at each valid state
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

BASE_URL = "http://localhost:8080/fhir"

def test_consent_transitions_active_to_inactive_state(patient_auth_token):
    hdrs = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {patient_auth_token}",
            "Content-Type": "application/fhir+json"}
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "Consent", "status": "proposed",
        "scope": {"coding": [{"code": "patient-privacy"}]},
        "category": [{"coding": [{"code": "59284-0"}]}],
        "patient": {"reference": "Patient/<test_patient_id>"},
        "provision": {"type": "permit"}
    }
    r1 = requests.post(f"{BASE_URL}/Consent", json=payload, headers=hdrs)
    assert r1.status_code == 201
    cid = r1.json()["id"]
    active_payload = {**r1.json(), "status": "active"}
    r2 = requests.put(f"{BASE_URL}/Consent/{cid}", json=active_payload, headers=hdrs)
    assert r2.status_code == 200
    assert r2.json()["status"] == "active"
    patch_hdrs = {**hdrs, "Content-Type": "application/json-patch+json"}
    r3 = requests.patch(f"{BASE_URL}/Consent/{cid}",
                        json=[{"op": "replace", "path": "/status", "value": "inactive"}],
                        headers=patch_hdrs)
    assert r3.status_code in (200, 204)
    r4 = requests.get(f"{BASE_URL}/Consent/{cid}", headers=hdrs)
    assert r4.json()["status"] == "inactive"
    requests.delete(f"{BASE_URL}/Consent/{cid}", headers=hdrs)
